SEARCH
Package simulator.model

Class UserProcess

java.lang.Object
simulator.model.Process
simulator.model.UserProcess
public class UserProcess extends Process
Represents a process launched by a user within the system. Unlike a system process, a UserProcess is defined by a release time and an alternating execution sequence consisting of processing intervals (CPU) and system calls (I/O).

  • Field Summary

  • Constructor Details

    • UserProcess

      public UserProcess(int id, int memory, int releaseTime, int[] sequence)
      Constructs a new user process based on data read from the file.
      Parameters:
      id - Unique identifier of the process.
      memory - RAM memory required (in MB).
      releaseTime - The time (tick) when the process appears in the system.
      sequence - Array with the alternating duration sequence (CPU, IO, CPU...).

  • Method Details

    • getReleaseTime

      public int getReleaseTime()
      Returns the time at which the process is released (introduced) into the system.
      Returns:
      Release time.

    • isCurrentlyDoingIo

      public boolean isCurrentlyDoingIo()
      Checks if the current stage of the process is a system call. This information helps the Scheduler know if the process needs to be removed from the processor and handled by the system process.
      Returns:
      true if the process is waiting/executing I/O, false if it requires CPU.

    • executeTick

      public void executeTick(int currentTime)
      Executes one time unit (one tick) of the process's current interval. The function manually updates the state and moves to the next interval in the array when the current time reaches 0. If the array ends, the process transitions to TERMINATED.
      Specified by:
      executeTick in class Process
      Parameters:
      currentTime - Current global simulation time.

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a text representation of the process for debugging and logging.
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      Formatted string with process details.
